annotations.label: Elliptic curve (genus one with point O)

anchor.snippet: an elliptic curve is a smooth , projective , algebraic curve of genus one

annotations.note: Mathlib defines an elliptic curve as a Weierstrass curve with unit discriminant, a concrete model rather than the abstract smooth projective genus-one curve with a point.

annotations.label: Non-singularity condition

anchor.snippet: The curve is required to be non-singular , which means that the curve has no cusps or self-intersections .

annotations.note: WeierstrassCurve.Affine.Nonsingular is exactly the non-vanishing-partial-derivatives nonsingularity condition at an affine point.

annotations.label: Elliptic curve is an abelian variety

anchor.snippet: An elliptic curve is an abelian variety

annotations.note: Mathlib has no notion of abelian variety to identify an elliptic curve with.

annotations.label: y² = P(x), deg 3, distinct roots gives elliptic curve

anchor.snippet: the solution set is a nonsingular plane curve of genus one

annotations.note: Mathlib proves Δ ≠ 0 (distinct roots) forces every solution point to be nonsingular, but the genus-one conclusion is not formalized.

annotations.label: Complex elliptic curves correspond to torus embeddings

anchor.snippet: elliptic curves defined over the complex numbers correspond to embeddings of the torus into the complex projective plane

annotations.note: No construction of the embedding ℂ/Λ → ℙ²(ℂ) exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Real elliptic curves as ellipses in hyperbolic plane

anchor.snippet: there is a natural representation of real elliptic curves with shape invariant j ≥ 1 as ellipses in the hyperbolic plane

annotations.note: No such hyperbolic-plane representation of real elliptic curves is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Topology: elliptic curve is a torus

anchor.snippet: a complex elliptic curve is a torus , while a complex ellipse is a sphere

annotations.note: The topological homeomorphism of a complex elliptic curve with a torus is not formalized.

annotations.label: Weierstrass normal form

anchor.section: Elliptic curves over the real numbers
anchor.snippet: an elliptic curve is a plane curve defined by an equation of the form

annotations.note: WeierstrassCurve.IsShortNF is exactly the short Weierstrass form Y² = X³ + a₄X + a₆.

annotations.label: Non-singular iff discriminant nonzero

anchor.section: Elliptic curves over the real numbers
anchor.snippet: Algebraically, this holds if and only if the discriminant

annotations.note: Mathlib proves the Δ ≠ 0 ⟹ nonsingular direction (and defines IsElliptic via IsUnit Δ), but not the full biconditional with discriminant as in the article.

annotations.label: Number of real components from discriminant sign

anchor.section: Elliptic curves over the real numbers
anchor.snippet: The real graph of a non-singular curve has two components if its discriminant is positive

annotations.note: The number of real connected components as a function of the discriminant sign is not formalized.

annotations.label: Discriminants 64 and −368

anchor.section: Elliptic curves over the real numbers
anchor.snippet: the discriminant in the first case is 64, and in the second case is −368

annotations.note: These specific numeric discriminant examples are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Point at infinity is unique intersection with line at infinity

anchor.snippet: which is thus the unique intersection of the curve with the line at infinity

annotations.note: The unique point at infinity is modeled as the single `zero` constructor of Point, but the projective intersection-with-line-at-infinity statement is not proven.

annotations.label: Point at infinity is group identity

anchor.snippet: this point at infinity is the identity element of a group structure

annotations.note: In the AddCommGroup instance the point at infinity `Point.zero` is the additive identity (zero_add/add_zero).

annotations.label: Inverse −P as opposite point

anchor.snippet: we can take − P to be the point opposite it

annotations.note: Point.neg defines −P as the third intersection with the vertical line through P.

annotations.label: Addition P + Q via chord

anchor.snippet: then we can uniquely describe a third point P + Q in the following way

annotations.note: Point.add defines P + Q via the secant/tangent (chord) construction.

annotations.label: Special cases of the addition law

anchor.snippet: This definition for addition works except in a few special cases related to the point at infinity and intersection multiplicity

annotations.note: The add definition explicitly handles the point-at-infinity and y₁ = negY (vertical line) special cases via case analysis.

annotations.label: K-rational points

anchor.snippet: the K - rational points of E are the points on E whose coordinates all lie in K

annotations.note: WeierstrassCurve.Affine.Point over a field F is exactly the set of F-rational points (nonsingular points with coordinates in F, plus infinity).

annotations.label: E(K) is a group

anchor.snippet: E ( K ) is a group, because properties of polynomial equations show

annotations.note: The nonsingular points form an AddCommGroup under the chord-tangent law.

annotations.label: E(K) is a subgroup of E(L)

anchor.snippet: if K is a subfield of L , then E ( K ) is a subgroup of E ( L )

annotations.note: Point.map is an additive group homomorphism E(K) →+ E(L) induced by a field extension, and map_injective shows it embeds E(K) as a subgroup.

annotations.label: Addition formula, distinct x (case 1)

anchor.snippet: assume first that x P ≠ x Q

annotations.note: slope_of_X_ne gives slope (y₁−y₂)/(x₁−x₂), combined with addX/addY for the secant addition formula.

annotations.label: Inverse by reflection across x-axis (cases 3,4)

anchor.snippet: the inverse of each point on the curve is found by reflecting it across the x axis

annotations.note: negY = −y − a₁x − a₃ gives the inverse's y-coordinate; reflection y ↦ −y is the special case a₁ = a₃ = 0.

annotations.label: Doubling formula via tangent (case 2)

anchor.snippet: The slope is given by the tangent to the curve at

annotations.note: slope_of_Y_ne gives the tangent-line slope (3x²+2a₂x+a₄−a₁y)/(2y+a₁x+a₃) used for point doubling.

annotations.label: General slope expression for cases 1 and 2

anchor.snippet: that works in both case 1 and case 2 is

annotations.note: The single `slope` definition covers both the secant and tangent cases.

annotations.label: Addition formulas in characteristic 3

anchor.snippet: the general form of an elliptic curve with characteristic 3

annotations.note: The characteristic-3 normal form is formalized and the general affine addition formulas apply, but no explicit char-3 addition formulas are stated separately.

annotations.label: Group law on general cubic via inflection point

anchor.snippet: we can still define a group structure by designating one of its nine inflection points as the identity O

annotations.note: Mathlib only has the group law on Weierstrass models, not on a general plane cubic via a chosen inflection point.

annotations.label: Group law on Hessian curves

anchor.snippet: For an example of the group law over a non-Weierstrass curve, see Hessian curves

annotations.note: Hessian curves and their group law are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Rational points form a subgroup of real points

anchor.section: Elliptic curves over the rational numbers
anchor.snippet: the set of rational points of E forms a subgroup of the group of real points of E

annotations.note: The injective base-change homomorphism Point.map specializes to E(ℚ) →+ E(ℝ), realizing E(ℚ) as a subgroup.

annotations.label: y² = x³ + 17 integral solutions

anchor.snippet: has eight integral solutions with y > 0

annotations.note: This specific enumeration of integral points is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Ljunggren's equation solutions

anchor.snippet: has only four solutions with y ≥ 0

annotations.note: Ljunggren's equation and its solution count are not formalized.

annotations.label: Mordell–Weil theorem

anchor.snippet: the Mordell–Weil theorem states that the group E ( Q ) is a finitely generated (abelian) group

annotations.note: The Mordell–Weil theorem is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Structure via fundamental theorem of fg abelian groups

anchor.snippet: it is therefore a finite direct sum of copies of Z and finite cyclic groups

annotations.note: The general structure theorem for finitely generated abelian groups is formalized, but its application to E(ℚ) depends on the unformalized Mordell–Weil theorem.

annotations.label: Weak Mordell–Weil theorem

anchor.snippet: the quotient group E ( Q )/ mE ( Q ) is finite (this is the weak Mordell–Weil theorem)

annotations.note: The weak Mordell–Weil theorem is not formalized.

annotations.label: Finiteness of bounded-height rational points

anchor.snippet: only finitely many rational points with height smaller than any constant exist on E

annotations.note: Heights of points on elliptic curves and their finiteness are not formalized.

annotations.label: Rank of E(Q)

anchor.snippet: The rank of E ( Q ) is the number of copies of Z in E ( Q )

annotations.note: The Mordell–Weil rank of an elliptic curve is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Curve of rank 20

anchor.snippet: It has rank 20, found by Noam Elkies and Zev Klagsbrun in 2020

annotations.note: This record rank example is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Mazur's torsion theorem

anchor.snippet: the torsion subgroup of E ( Q ) is one of the 15 following groups ( a theorem due to Barry Mazur )

annotations.note: Mazur's classification of torsion subgroups is not formalized.

annotations.label: Birch and Swinnerton-Dyer conjecture

anchor.snippet: The Birch and Swinnerton-Dyer conjecture (BSD) is one of the Millennium problems of the Clay Mathematics Institute

annotations.note: The BSD conjecture is not stated in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hasse–Weil zeta function

anchor.snippet: the Hasse–Weil zeta function of E over Q

annotations.note: Mathlib defines the L-function via local Euler factors, closely related to but not identical with the Hasse–Weil zeta function.

annotations.label: Bad reduction at p

anchor.snippet: in which case E is said to be of bad reduction at p

annotations.note: Mathlib defines good, multiplicative, and additive reduction; bad reduction is the complement of HasGoodReduction but has no standalone predicate.

annotations.label: Zeta function over a finite field

anchor.snippet: The zeta function of an elliptic curve over a finite field F p is, in some sense, a generating function

annotations.note: localPolynomial gives the numerator 1 − aT + qT² of the local zeta function, but the exp-generating-series zeta function over a finite field is not defined.

annotations.label: Zeta function is rational in T

anchor.snippet: the so-defined zeta function is a rational function in T

annotations.note: No theorem asserting rationality of the elliptic-curve zeta function is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Trace of Frobenius

anchor.snippet: the 'trace of Frobenius' term

annotations.note: The quantity aₚ = q + 1 − |E(κ)| appears inside localPolynomial but there is no standalone trace-of-Frobenius definition.

annotations.label: L-function of E over Q

anchor.snippet: The L-function of E over Q is then defined by collecting this information together, for all primes p

annotations.note: WeierstrassCurve.LFunction (and LSeries) defines the L-function over any number field via the Euler product of local factors, with ℚ as a special case.

annotations.label: Conductor of E

anchor.snippet: where N is the conductor of E , i.e. the product of primes with bad reduction

annotations.note: The conductor of an elliptic curve is not defined in Mathlib (existing `conductor` decls are unrelated ring/character conductors).

annotations.label: Bad reduction at 17

anchor.snippet: has bad reduction at 17, because

annotations.note: This specific bad-reduction example is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hasse's conjecture (analytic continuation)

anchor.snippet: Hasse's conjecture affirms that the L -function admits an analytic continuation to the whole complex plane

annotations.note: Analytic continuation of the elliptic-curve L-function is not formalized.

annotations.label: Shimura–Taniyama–Weil (modularity)

anchor.snippet: which asserts that every elliptic curve over Q is a modular curve

annotations.note: The modularity theorem is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: BSD: vanishing order equals rank

anchor.snippet: the vanishing order of the L -function at s = 1 equals the rank of E

annotations.note: The BSD rank/order-of-vanishing statement is not formalized.

annotations.label: Congruent number

anchor.snippet: A congruent number is defined as an odd square-free integer n which is the area of a right triangle with rational side lengths

annotations.note: Congruent numbers are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Congruent number iff rational point of infinite order

anchor.snippet: has a rational point of infinite order

annotations.note: The congruent-number criterion via infinite-order rational points is not formalized.

annotations.label: Tunnell's theorem

anchor.snippet: Tunnell has shown a related result: assuming BSD

annotations.note: Tunnell's theorem is not formalized.

annotations.label: Average rank under GRH and BSD

anchor.snippet: assuming the generalized Riemann hypothesis and BSD

annotations.note: Average-rank results for elliptic curves are not formalized.

annotations.label: Hasse's theorem on elliptic curves

anchor.snippet: Hasse's theorem on elliptic curves gives the following inequality

annotations.note: Hasse's bound |#E(F_q) − (q+1)| ≤ 2√q is not formalized.

annotations.label: E(F_q) is cyclic or product of two cyclic groups

anchor.snippet: It is always cyclic or the product of two cyclic groups

annotations.note: The structure of E(F_q) as cyclic or a product of two cyclic groups is not formalized.

annotations.label: Curve over F71 with 72 points

anchor.snippet: over F 71 has 72 points

annotations.note: This specific point-count example is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Local zeta function of E over F_q

anchor.snippet: the local zeta function of E over F q , defined by a generating series

annotations.note: localPolynomial provides the local factor 1 − aT + qT², but the full local zeta generating series is not defined.

annotations.label: Local zeta function is rational in T

anchor.snippet: The zeta function is a rational function in T

annotations.note: No rationality theorem for the local zeta function is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Zeta function of y²+y=x³ over F2

anchor.snippet: the zeta function of E : y 2 + y = x 3 over the field F 2 is given by

annotations.note: This specific zeta-function computation is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Functional equation of zeta function

anchor.snippet: The functional equation is

annotations.note: The functional equation of the elliptic-curve zeta function is not formalized.

annotations.label: Sato–Tate conjecture

anchor.snippet: The Sato–Tate conjecture is a statement about how the error term

annotations.note: The Sato–Tate conjecture is not formalized.

annotations.label: Elliptic curve over a general field

anchor.section: Elliptic curves over a general field
anchor.snippet: the formal definition of an elliptic curve is a non-singular projective algebraic curve over K with genus 1

annotations.note: Mathlib's IsElliptic over a field captures elliptic curves via the Weierstrass model (unit discriminant), not the abstract genus-one projective curve definition.

anchor.section: Elliptic curves over a general field
anchor.snippet: in characteristic 3, the most general equation is of the form

annotations.note: exists_variableChange_isCharThreeNF reduces any curve over a char-3 field to the IsCharThreeNF normal form.

anchor.section: Elliptic curves over a general field

annotations.label: K-rational points (general field)

anchor.section: Elliptic curves over a general field
anchor.snippet: Points of the curve whose coordinates both belong to K are called K -rational points

annotations.note: WeierstrassCurve.Affine.Point over a field K is exactly the set of K-rational points.

annotations.label: Mordell–Weil over a number field

anchor.section: Elliptic curves over a general field
anchor.snippet: the group E(K) of K -rational points of an elliptic curve E defined over K is finitely generated

annotations.note: The Mordell–Weil theorem over number fields is not formalized.

annotations.label: Merel's theorem on torsion

anchor.section: Elliptic curves over a general field
anchor.snippet: A theorem due to Loïc Merel shows that for a given integer d

annotations.note: Merel's uniform boundedness theorem is not formalized.

annotations.label: Siegel's theorem on integral points

anchor.section: Elliptic curves over a general field
anchor.snippet: Then there are only finitely many points of E(K) whose x -coordinate is in the ring of integers O K

annotations.note: Siegel's theorem on finiteness of integral points is not formalized (Mathlib's Siegel's lemma is unrelated).

annotations.label: Weierstrass ℘ differential relation

anchor.section: Elliptic curves over the complex numbers
anchor.snippet: These functions and their first derivative are related by the formula

annotations.note: derivWeierstrassP_sq proves ℘'(z)² = 4℘(z)³ − g₂℘(z) − g₃.

annotations.label: Weierstrass functions doubly periodic on torus

anchor.section: Elliptic curves over the complex numbers
anchor.snippet: The Weierstrass functions are doubly periodic

annotations.note: weierstrassP_add_coe (and derivWeierstrassP_add_coe) prove ℘ and ℘' are invariant under lattice translations, i.e. doubly periodic.

annotations.label: Embedding map is group isomorphism

anchor.section: Elliptic curves over the complex numbers
anchor.snippet: This map is a group isomorphism of the torus

annotations.note: The group isomorphism ℂ/Λ ≅ E(ℂ) via (℘, ℘') is not constructed in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Scaled lattices give isomorphic curves

anchor.section: Elliptic curves over the complex numbers
anchor.snippet: then the corresponding curves are isomorphic

annotations.note: The statement that homothetic lattices yield isomorphic complex curves is not formalized.

annotations.label: Isomorphism classes by j-invariant

anchor.section: Elliptic curves over the complex numbers
anchor.snippet: Isomorphism classes of elliptic curves are specified by the j -invariant

annotations.note: Over a separably closed field, equal j-invariants imply the curves are related by a change of variables, generalizing the ℂ case.

annotations.label: Modular invariants g2, g3

anchor.section: Elliptic curves over the complex numbers
anchor.snippet: The constants g 2 and g 3 , called the modular invariants , are uniquely determined by the lattice

annotations.note: PeriodPair.g₂ = 60·G₄ and PeriodPair.g₃ = 140·G₆ are the lattice invariants, matching the article's definitions.

annotations.label: j(2i) = 287496

anchor.section: Elliptic curves over the complex numbers
anchor.snippet: it yields the integer j (2 i ) = 66 3 = 287 496

annotations.note: This specific value of the analytic j-function is not computed in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Modular discriminant is transcendental

anchor.section: Elliptic curves over the complex numbers
anchor.snippet: is generally a transcendental number

annotations.note: Transcendence of the modular discriminant is not formalized.

annotations.label: Uniformization theorem for genus-one surfaces

anchor.section: Elliptic curves over the complex numbers
anchor.snippet: the uniformization theorem implies that every compact Riemann surface of genus one can be represented as a torus

annotations.note: The uniformization theorem for genus-one Riemann surfaces is not formalized.

anchor.section: Elliptic curves over the complex numbers
anchor.snippet: then the n -torsion points are the (equivalence classes of) points of the form

annotations.note: The explicit description of n-torsion of ℂ/Λ in the elliptic-curve setting is not formalized.

anchor.section: Elliptic curves over the complex numbers
anchor.snippet: a pair of fundamental periods of E can be calculated very rapidly by

annotations.note: Computing periods via the arithmetic–geometric mean is not formalized.

annotations.label: Nine inflection points and Hesse configuration

anchor.section: Elliptic curves over the complex numbers
anchor.snippet: Over the complex numbers, every elliptic curve has nine inflection points

annotations.note: The nine inflection points and Hesse configuration are not formalized.

annotations.label: Dual isogeny

anchor.snippet: the dual isogeny is an isogeny

annotations.note: Isogenies of elliptic curves are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Explicit construction of dual isogeny

anchor.snippet: it can be explicitly given as the composition

annotations.note: No construction of the dual isogeny exists in Mathlib.
